assertions.title: Error Handling Displays Toast

steps.observed: Console shows 500 errors: 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500'

steps.expected: Toast notification appears with error message
steps.observed: No toast notification appeared - notification region (role='region') was empty
Album 'Folge 152: Skateboardfieber' (2012) remained visible - state preserved correctly

consoleErrors: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error) - appeared4 times
evidence.network: No requests captured in agent-browser network log (likely due to timing)

assertions.issues: Toast notification did NOT appear when API error occurred. The notification region (role='region' with aria-label 'Notifications') remained empty after the error. However, the previous album state was correctly preserved - 'Folge 152: Skateboardfieber' remained visible after the failed reroll request.

frictions.description: Console errors showed 500 Internal Server Error instead of network failure - this is acceptable as both represent API errors, but the validation contract mentioned 'network failure or no albums'.
frictions.resolution: Killed backend process entirely to simulate network-unavailable scenario. The app correctly detected the failure and logged console errors.

summary: Tested VAL-CROSS-009: FAIL. When an API error occurred (backend killed, returning 500 errors), NO toast notification appeared. The notification region remained empty. However, the previous album was correctly preserved (state maintained). The assertion requires BOTH a toast notification AND state preservation - since the toast did not appear, this test FAILS.
